items.headline: Warren Buffett says Iran bomb would make nuclear disaster harder to avoid
items.summary: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman said the growing number of nuclear-armed states has fundamentally altered the global risk landscape.

items.headline: Lilly to spend up to $7.8 billion to acquire Centessa, a maker of experimental sleep disorder drugs
items.summary: Centessa is one of several companies working on a new class of drugs to treat narcolepsy, a condition that makes it difficult for people to stay awake.

items.headline: Oracle cutting thousands in latest layoff round as company continues to ramp AI spending
items.summary: Oracle has ratcheted up its capital expenditures as it builds data center infrastructure that can handle AI workloads.

items.headline: Social Security needs money to fix its shortfall. The question is, who will pay?
items.summary: Social Security's retirement trust fund may run out of money in 2032, according to the latest projections. Lawmakers have some ideas for how to fix the program.

items.headline: Snap climbs 13% as activist Irenic suggests changes to boost stock's value 7x
items.summary: Irenic Capital suggested shuttering the Specs division and replacing more than a fifth of Snap's workforce with artificial intelligence.

items.headline: Nike is set to report earnings after the bell. Here's what Wall Street expects
items.summary: Nike has shown some gains in North America, its largest market, amid its turnaround, but the strength was offset by weakness in China.

items.headline: 'Project Hail Mary' is the box office proof point Amazon MGM has been waiting for
items.summary: Amazon and MGM promised to spend around $1 billion each year on theatrical releases, a figure that would fund between 12 and 15 films annually.

items.headline: Why $4 a gallon gas prices won’t trigger Fed interest rate hikes — and could lead to cuts

items.headline: Moscow is profiting from the Iran war for now — but experts say Russia's economy is in the 'death zone'

items.headline: Tiger Woods had opioids in pants after DUI rollover crash in Florida, police say

items.headline: ServiceNow’s stock is having its worst quarter on record. What comes next?
